channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Democrats seek a new leader with Minnesota’s DFL party chair in contention
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor and host Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the Democratic National Committee’s election of a new party chair and the Minnesotan who’s campaigning for the job. Later, a conversation with newly elected Minnesota Republican Party Chair Alex Plechash.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Top 25: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick unveils priority bills for Texas Senate
item.description: Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick has announced the first round of his top 40 priority bills for the 89th Texas legislative session, outlining a conservative agenda that includes school choice, banning THC, and placing the Ten Commandments in schools.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal court rules ATF age limits on handgun sales violate Second Amendment
item.description: A three-judge panel of the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in New Orleans found a federal law that prohibits adults aged 18-20 from buying handguns violates the Second Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After stinging election defeats, DNC eyes rural voters as key to 2026 midterm success
item.description: The new strategy, previewed exclusively to Fox News Digital, seeks to hold President Donald Trump accountable for policies that Democrats say are harmful to rural voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the NTSB and the investigation into the DC plane crash
item.description: A deadly midair collision between an American Airlines jet and an Army helicopter near the nation's capital is bringing renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conspiracies, espionage, an enemies list: Takeaways from a wild day of confirmation hearings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conspiracy theories about vaccines. Secret meetings with dictators. An enemies list. President Donald Trump’ s most controversial Cabinet nominees — Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Senate advances Trump's energy secretary nominee to final confirmation vote
item.description: President Donald Trump's pick to head the Department of Energy, Chris Wright, has advanced to a final confirmation vote in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly DC plane crash begins
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump sounds the same. His White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The marathon Q&A sessions are back, along with the cream Oval Office rug and the Diet Coke button on the Resolute Desk. So, too, are the late-night social media posts that ricochet across the globe, the barrage of executive orders and press conferences filled with falsehoods.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. on defensive over his vaccine views as a key confirmation vote hangs in the balance
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr. 's bid to be the nation's top health official is uncertain after a key Republican joined Democrats to raise persistent concerns over the nominee's deep skepticism of routine childhood vaccinations that prevent deadly diseases. Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big Oil wants a lot from Trump. It has an ally in Doug Burgum, the president's Interior pick
item.description: Emails obtained by The Associated Press reveal Doug Burgum as North Dakota governor catered to the whims of oil and gas executives while leveraging those connections to expand his political profile.
item.dc:creator: Brian Slodysko And Jack Dura, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Watch live: Gabbard makes case to lead national intelligence at confirmation hearing
item.description: Former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (D-Hawaii) is testifying Thursday morning before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence to make her case as President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Trump takes aim at DEI after plane crash; Gabbard pressed on Snowden
item.description: President Trump on Thursday appeared to blame diversity initiatives at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for weakening safety following a deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C. Over two dozen bodies have been pulled from the water.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests FAA's diversity efforts caused safety issues in crash
item.description: Trump weighed in on a crash between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter, claiming it “should have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Despite Trump immigration policy changes, experts say the H-2A program is on solid footing
item.description: The Trump administration has started its overhaul of existing immigration policy. However, one program that allows migrant agricultural workers to work temporarily is for now, unaffected.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right health recommendations
item.description: The latest KFF survey finds 42 percent of respondents say they have either a great deal or fair amount of trust in Trump to make the right recommendations when it comes to health issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: A Democratic Party in crisis will elect its new chairman on Saturday
item.description: The Democratic National Committee will elect a new chair Saturday as it tries to guide Democrats through Republican Donald Trump’s second presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Center for Victims of Torture dramatically slashes workforce after Trump administration orders
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders forced many international humanitarian organizations, like St. Paul-based Center for Victims of Torture, to cut spending and furlough staff.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Frey formally launches bid for third term as Minneapolis mayor
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said Wednesday he’ll seek a third — and final — term as mayor to continue his administration’s work overhauling the police department and building more affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
